title: “Yah, boo, sucks.” On the time Angela Carter absolutely flamed Joan Didion in an interview.
description: Here’s a fun fact to celebrate Angela Carter’s birthday: the beloved feminist icon did not care for literary tote saint Joan Didion. Need some proof? In 1986, Angela Carter sat down for a phone interview with Rosemary Carroll for BOMB.
hook: Angela Carter absolutely eviscerated Joan Didion in 1986, and the transcript proves she meant every word of it.

title: My Millenarian Year
description:
‘We believed the world would undergo radical change in 2012 and Adam, and Adam alone, could guide us through.’

Ralf Webb on charismatic leaders and the end of the world. 

The post My Millenarian Year appeared first on Granta.
hook: He joined a doomsday cult in 2012 because one charismatic man promised to guide them through the apocalypse. This is what he learned.

title: I Wasn’t Excited for My Top Surgery. That Doesn’t Negate My Desire for It
description:
Minor Meats by Billy Lezra The right one weighs 568 grams, the left one, 547, over two pounds off my chest. For five days, two tubes drain the incisions. Ruby, then amber, fluids pool into translucent bulbs pinned to my white compression vest. It’s Christmas.  I am the tree; the blood bulbs, ornaments.  Inside the […]

The post I Wasn’t Excited for My Top Su
hook: Top surgery felt like removing his chest piece by piece. The way his body shed itself into translucent bulbs is both brutal and beautiful.

title: The magnificent range of Marianne Jean-Baptiste
description: As Secrets & Lies turns 30, we look back on Jean-Baptiste's world-class collaborations with Mike Leigh.
hook: Marianne Jean-Baptiste's collaborations with Mike Leigh prove she's one of cinema's most fearlessly intelligent actors. Here's why we keep missing it.

title: James Schuyler, the New York school's most neglected poet, may now be its most necessary
description: James Schuyler, the New York school's most neglected poet, may now be its most necessary
hook: James Schuyler was poetry's invisible man. Now his neglected work reads like urgent prophecy for a fractured moment.
